questions.q: Of which kingdom was Janaka the king?
questions.explanation: Janaka was the renowned king of Mithila, the kingdom of the Videhas.

questions.q: Who was Janaka's celebrated daughter?
questions.explanation: Sita, the wife of Rama, was the daughter of King Janaka.

questions.q: How did Janaka discover the infant Sita?
questions.explanation: Janaka found the infant Sita in a furrow while ploughing the earth during a ritual.

questions.q: What was set as the condition for winning Sita's hand in marriage?
questions.explanation: Janaka decreed that only the suitor who could string and break Shiva's mighty bow would marry Sita.

questions.q: Who ultimately broke Shiva's bow and won Sita's hand?
questions.explanation: Rama lifted and broke Shiva's bow, fulfilling the condition to marry Sita.

questions.q: Janaka is best remembered as what kind of ruler?
options: A wandering ascetic with no kingdom
questions.explanation: Janaka is celebrated as the wise, detached philosopher-king of Mithila.

questions.q: What was the relationship of Janaka to Rama after the wedding?
questions.explanation: By giving his daughter Sita in marriage to Rama, Janaka became Rama's father-in-law.

questions.q: Because Sita was found in the earth and raised by Janaka, she is also known by which name?
questions.explanation: Sita is called Janaki, meaning 'daughter of Janaka.'

questions.q: Whose bow did the suitors attempt to lift at Sita's swayamvara?
questions.explanation: The bow at the contest was the divine bow of Shiva, kept in Janaka's treasury.

questions.q: What activity was Janaka performing when he found Sita?
questions.explanation: Janaka was ploughing the field as part of a ritual when the infant Sita emerged from the furrow.

questions.q: By what dynastic name were the kings of Mithila, including Janaka, known?
questions.explanation: The rulers of Mithila were called the Videhas, and Janaka bore this lineage title.

questions.q: Which sage brought Rama and Lakshmana to Janaka's court at Mithila?
questions.explanation: Sage Vishvamitra escorted Rama and Lakshmana to Mithila, where they saw Shiva's bow.

questions.q: Sita is also called Ayonija, which means what?
questions.explanation: Ayonija means 'not born from a womb,' reflecting that Sita arose from the earth, not natural birth.

questions.q: Besides Sita, Janaka raised another daughter who married Lakshmana. What was her name?
questions.explanation: Urmila, Janaka's daughter, was wedded to Lakshmana at the same celebration.

questions.q: Janaka's younger brother, who ruled a neighboring realm, had two daughters who also married into Dasharatha's family. What was his name?
questions.explanation: Kushadhvaja, Janaka's brother, was the father of Mandavi and Shrutakirti, who wed Bharata and Shatrughna.

questions.q: Who was the chief priest and counselor at Janaka's court in Mithila?
questions.explanation: Satananda, son of the sage Gautama and Ahalya, served as the family priest of Janaka.

questions.q: From whom did Janaka's lineage originally receive Shiva's great bow?
options: It was forged by Janaka himself
It was given by the gods to an ancestor of Janaka
It was a gift from Rama
questions.explanation: The bow of Shiva was entrusted by the gods to Janaka's ancestor Devarata and kept in Mithila for generations.

questions.q: What reaction did Janaka have on first hearing that Shiva's bow had been broken?
options: He demanded the bow be repaired
He was overjoyed that a worthy suitor was found
questions.explanation: Janaka rejoiced, for breaking the bow proved Rama worthy to wed Sita.

questions.q: After the bow was broken, whom did Janaka send messengers to invite for the wedding?
questions.explanation: Janaka sent envoys to Ayodhya to invite Dasharatha and his family for the marriage of Sita to Rama.

questions.q: Janaka's name became almost a title for the philosopher-kings of Mithila. What did such a king embody?
options: Royal duty combined with spiritual wisdom
questions.explanation: Janaka exemplified a ruler who governed with detachment while pursuing the highest spiritual knowledge.

questions.q: Which strong suitors had earlier failed even to lift Shiva's bow before Rama arrived?
questions.explanation: Numerous kings and princes had tried and failed to lift the bow, making Rama's feat all the more remarkable.

questions.q: How is Sita's connection to the earth reflected in another of her epithets?
options: She is called Agniputri, daughter of fire
She is called Vayuputri, daughter of wind
She is called Suryaja, daughter of the sun
She is called Bhumija, daughter of the Earth
questions.explanation: Because Janaka found her in the soil, Sita is called Bhumija, 'daughter of the Earth.'

questions.q: In later Upanishadic tradition, King Janaka of Mithila is famously associated with receiving teachings from which sage?
questions.explanation: Janaka is renowned in the Brihadaranyaka Upanishad for his dialogues with the sage Yajnavalkya.

questions.q: The ritual ploughing during which Janaka found Sita was performed for what purpose?
options: To plant the year's first crop for profit
To consecrate the field for a sacrifice
questions.explanation: Janaka was ploughing to purify and consecrate the sacrificial ground when Sita appeared from the furrow.

questions.q: What was the personal name traditionally given to this Janaka, distinguishing him in the line of Mithila kings?
questions.explanation: This Janaka was named Seeradhwaja (Siradhvaja), meaning 'he whose emblem is the plough.'

questions.q: How many of Dasharatha's sons were married together at Janaka's court in Mithila?
questions.explanation: All four of Dasharatha's sons were wed at Mithila to Sita, Urmila, Mandavi, and Shrutakirti.

questions.q: Which fierce warrior-sage confronted Rama after the wedding party left Mithila, angered by the breaking of Shiva's bow?
questions.explanation: Parashurama, devotee of Shiva, challenged Rama on the road from Mithila over the broken bow.

questions.q: The name of Janaka's kingdom, 'Videha,' is often interpreted to signify what quality of its kings?
options: Detachment from the body and worldly bonds
questions.explanation: 'Videha' suggests being unattached to the body, fitting Janaka's reputation for spiritual detachment.

questions.q: According to tradition, what motivated Janaka to set such a difficult bride-price as breaking Shiva's bow?
options: To avoid marrying Sita to anyone
To ensure only a hero of supreme strength and virtue could marry Sita
To start a war among kings
questions.explanation: Janaka required the feat so that only a truly worthy and mighty suitor would be united with his extraordinary daughter.

questions.q: When Rama broke Shiva's bow, what notable phenomenon accompanied the event?
options: A total eclipse of the sun
A thunderous sound that shook the assembly
questions.explanation: The bow snapped with a thunderous crash so loud that it stunned and frightened the gathered assembly.
